specs.body:
# Spec 0000: Platform

## Status

Stable

## Purpose

Define PeripheralOS as a platform for programmable peripherals.

## Scope

- platform identity
- governance boundaries
- contract hierarchy
- implementation boundaries

## Non-Goals

- device-specific behavior
- application-specific behavior
- transport implementation details

# Spec 0001: Architecture

## Status

Stable

## Purpose

Define the top-level architecture of PeripheralOS.

## Layers

- normative
- evolution
- execution

## Requirements

- separate standards from implementations
- keep core device-agnostic
- keep contracts versioned
- keep ownership explicit

# Spec 0002: Peripheral ABI

## Status

Stable

## Purpose

Define the canonical contract used by all PeripheralOS devices.

## Sections

- identity
- capabilities
- sensors
- actions
- diagnostics
- extensions

## Requirements

- single source of truth
- versioned schema
- language-neutral
- generated bindings only

# Spec 0003: Runtime

## Status

Stable

## Purpose

Define the runtime responsibilities of PeripheralOS.

## Responsibilities

- lifecycle management
- configuration
- validation
- diagnostics
- plugin orchestration
- transport mediation

## Non-Goals

- device-specific rules
- application-specific rules

# Spec 0004: Device Model

## Status

Stable

## Purpose

Define what a device is in PeripheralOS.

## Device Requirements

- identity
- capabilities
- lifecycle
- diagnostics
- health
- extension support

# Spec 0005: Plugin Model

## Status

Stable

## Purpose

Define how plugins integrate with PeripheralOS.

## Lifecycle

- load
- initialize
- advertise capabilities
- run
- health check
- shutdown

# Spec 0006: Action Model

## Status

Stable

## Purpose

Define the abstract action vocabulary used by PeripheralOS.

## Examples

- primary action
- secondary action
- aim
- interact
- navigate
- confirm
- cancel
- rotate
- translate
- zoom

# Spec 0007: Transport Model

## Status

Stable

## Purpose

Define transport as a replaceable concern.

## Transport Examples

- UDP
- TCP
- BLE
- USB
- WebRTC
- Serial

# Spec 0008: Runtime Lifecycle

## Status

Stable

## Purpose

Define the canonical lifecycle for devices and plugins.

## Lifecycle

- discover
- pair
- authenticate
- handshake
- capabilities
- ready
- streaming
- disconnected
- reconnect

# Spec 0009: Security

## Status

Stable

## Purpose

Define the security model for PeripheralOS.

## Requirements

- explicit trust boundaries
- authenticated device pairing
- transport-aware validation
- least privilege by default
- no hidden architecture

# Spec 0010: Capabilities

## Status

Stable

## Purpose

Define how devices advertise what they can do.

## Capability Classes

- sensors
- actions
- diagnostics
- extensions
- transport support

# Spec 0011: Extension Model

## Status

Stable

## Purpose

Define how PeripheralOS grows without changing the Core.

## Requirements

- extensible without core changes
- versioned extension contracts
- explicit compatibility declarations

# Spec 0012: Generator

## Status

Frozen

## Purpose

Define the specification compiler that turns platform specs into a canonical AST and derived artifacts.

## Pipeline

- Parser
- Semantic Validation
- Platform IR
- Emitters
- Conformance
- Compatibility Reports

## Requirements

- deterministic output
- single source of truth
- no handwritten bindings
- no direct implementation-to-implementation generation

# Spec 0013: Canonical AST / Platform IR

## Status

Frozen

## Purpose

Define the canonical internal representation used by the generator to transform specifications into derived artifacts.

## Contract

The Platform IR is the semantic representation between parsing and emission.

It must be:

- versioned
- deterministic
- language-neutral
- stable enough for code generation and conformance

## Required Fields

- protocol
- index
- specs

## Required Spec Node Fields

- header
- body

## Validation Rules

- The IR protocol must be explicit.
- The IR must preserve spec ordering.
- The IR must derive from `platform/spec/index.yaml` and the spec files.
- No emitter may become a source of truth.
